reforms.description: New Zealand introduced a 15% research and development tax credit aimed at boosting productivity by increasing fiscal support for business R&D, replacing the previous Growth Grants system. This reform is designed to support innovation and technology diffusion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes businesses to invest in R&D, which is expected to enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been successfully introduced and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new tax credit system represents a significant overhaul of the previous funding mechanism for R&D.
reforms.source_quote: Strategic deployment of the Commerce Commission’s new market studies power and increased support through the new research and development tax credit should also serve to increase productivity.

reforms.description: The New Zealand Government is implementing a well-being approach to policy and budget decision-making, which includes developing well-being frameworks and indicator sets to inform budget priorities and decision-making.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the well-being approach on economic growth is not clearly defined and may vary based on implementation.
reforms.status_evidence: The well-being approach is actively being implemented in policy-making processes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how policy and budget decisions are made, focusing on well-being outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The New Zealand Government is applying a well-being approach to policy and budget decision-making with the objective of lifting New Zealanders’ well-being.
